description: Apollo 16 Flight Journal - Day 3, Part 1: ALFMED Experiment

tokens: Houston, how do you read Apollo 16?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: How are you doing this morning?
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I guess that sort of depends on what y'all can say about the old platform.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The gimbal lock and the coarse align that you had back at 38 plus 18 [GET] was caused by a CDU transient in the yaw axis.
The transient was induced when the TVC relay was disabled.
And this relay is normally enabled when the Optics is in Manual and disabled when you select P00, and that's kind of the situation that you were in when that occurred.
And Ken had just selected P00, and he had, prior to that, been using the Optics in Manual.
And they say that this type transient has been observed in CSM 117 [Apollo 17] testing, and we're presently looking at - a software work-around for LOI.
And we'll get some more words to you later on this - on this same subject.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: That's the best news I've heard today, so far.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, John, we've had some problems here getting commands up to the bird - real-time commands.
And we've got a command test we want to run.
As soon as you can get ready, let us know and we'll get into it.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: We've got some seat swap to do and we'll let you know.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This is Apollo Control; 46 hours, 1 minute Ground Elapsed Time, and about 30 minutes early, the - or ahead of the scheduled wakeup time, the crew has called Mission Control.
We have a few seconds of accumulated tape caught unawares.
Then we will rejoin the conversation with the crew of Apollo 16 live.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: 16, in the situation we're in now, we've only got one omni that we can read, and we can't command.
So we're only getting about 11 minutes of low-bit rate data out of every 18 while you're in PTC.
It's possible - we might lose you; if so, just stand by and we'll pick you up again.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: We're ready for that command test, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're gonna go one step at a time.
First thing we want to do is Up Telemetry, Command Reset and then Normal.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I can give you - You want a Command Reset and then Normal?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, we want the Up Telemetry Command switch to Reset and then Normal.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: But we want to cycle the Up Telemetry switch, Off, for three seconds and then back to Normal.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: And, Charlie, we've got commands again.
We'll handle the antenna and the bit rate for you now.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: To repeat an old phrase, Charlie, we had a lot of people down here turning blue on this - until that - until that last little situation got cured there.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Well, you could put Fendell in the backup mode and just put him up on top of the building.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We may have to put him up there anyway, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I've got about three - a couple, three updates to the Flight Plan.
Whenever you're - it's convenient for you, we'll read them up.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Don, you about ready for a little status report?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Casper we read you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Houston, if you're reading Casper, we're transmitting in the blind.
We have a signal strength on the meter of about 60 percent.
We're not receiving you and, based on your comment about the antennas, we'll wait about five minutes and then try again before we change the configuration.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We lost you there for a while there, Don.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You were loud and clear all the time.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: And, 16, the comm problem may have been on the ground.
We'll try to find out and let you know in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: The background tone at the - the background noise that the system is making right now is a little different than it was making before, if that would help you figure out what the problem was.
It's almost like it wasn't up-linking, maybe.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Don; and, if you're ready, I'll give you some status reports.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Stand by just a minute, Ken.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, Casper, go ahead with your status report.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I'm using that famous old trick on how to get someone to talk to you, but every time I pick up a juice bag and get halfway down, why it seems like we are able to establish comm.
So I'll start with the Commander's list and I'll go through and I'll skip the menus since they're on separate pages and come back to them.
So let's start with A-1: 22035; Alpha 3: 7, good; Alpha 4: none; Alpha 5: 19, 15, 18; Alpha 6: 6, 5, 6, and 3.
[Pause.] Charlie 1: 21059; Charlie 3: 7, good; Charlie 4: none; Charlie 5: 15, 30; Charlie 6: 7, 7, 5, 5, 5.
Okay, and if you want the menu stuff, I'll go back to that now.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: For John, and that's day 2, Meal B, scratch the bread and peanut butter.
[Pause.] On Meal C, scratch the frankfurters; add an orange drink.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Affirmative; we're getting a lot of background noise.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: [Pause.] Hold off a minute, 16; we'll switch omnis.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, starting on - Where did you copy last?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, we got John, day 2, Meal Charlie complete, and we're ready, I guess, for Bravo.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: On Meal A, you can scratch sausage patties and - orange juice - and fruit cocktail.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: On Bravo, that's Meal B, you can scratch turkey and gravy, vanilla pudding, bread, peanut butter.
Meal C, you can scratch the chocolate pudding.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: On meal A, scratch the sausage patties.
Meal B, you can scratch the bread and peanut butter.
Meal C, you can delete two of the frankfurters and scratch the chocolate pudding.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Casper, we copied all that.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, and a couple of other words here on the - We got ALFMED coming up; and, to date, Charlie saw some flashes the other night and not a whole heck of a lot of them last night.
And I'm not sure I have seen any at all yet, and John may or may not.
So, what we thought we'd do here is - We've got a little more house cleaning to do here and finish our chores.
And we'll probably take a look at what we see, and if Charlie is seeing sufficient - you know, enough flashes that he's pretty sure he's seeing things, why, we'll go and run the experiment now.

And if we aren't seeing a sufficient number to look like it justifies sitting here for an hour with it, why, what would you think about saving it until such time as it looks like maybe we see more of them?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We'll - we'll think on that a little.
And also, I've got a note on the Nikon camera light meter here in connection with the ALFMED photography.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Ken, you don't really need to copy much, I think.
If you've got the camera handy there where you can look at it, I can give it to you in a few words.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: You know one of the things we need in this program is some octopuses.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Don, I don't have the camera out, but how about just reading to me whatever it is you want to say, and I'll write it down here.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: It's not - it's probably not worth writing down except maybe a little note to remind you the - the camera was observed to hang up in the Battery Check position full down, when you hit the camera light meter On, Check.

And what we want you to do is - the little button on top of the camera there has a while band around the base of it - they want to make sure that white line is visible on the light meter switch button.
If it's not visible, you can take your fingernail and catch the top of that button and pull out on it until the white line is visible.
And then, verify that the meter is operating by holding the camera up to a light and observing the meter response to varying light levels.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: We'll make sure it works before we use it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That's all I've got on that one.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Flash, with a hot report here.
Pass on to the chefs that the grits were delicious.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I say, pass on to the chef that the grits were delicious.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: [Pause.] And for Ken's information, the status report that we just got was excellent as far as format, readability, and everything.
The - Doc said he really appreciates it.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: This is Apollo Control; 47 hours and 27 minutes into the mission of Apollo 16.
Crew awake now, presumably conducting either the ALFMED experiment or beyond the eat period.

As a matter of fact, part of their first activities after wakeup was the status report, which [lists] all the parts not eaten; in other words, negative reporting on the meals for the three crewmen are read down.
Also the problems with the commanding through the omni antennas have cleared themselves up, with the crew cycling a switch Off, back On again, which cleared the logic in the switching circuit.
And everything is properly working now in that area.

As soon as the crew completes the current housekeeping activities, the trouble shooting procedures for the Inertial Measuring Unit in the Guidance and Control System - Guidance and Navigation System will be read up to the crew, and they'll pursue that problem.

As a little aside to the crew food report, Charlie Duke, a good southern boy from South Carolina, said "pass on to the chef that the grits were delicious," grits having been packed in the menu on his request.
Present distance of Apollo 16, 157,253 nautical miles [291,233 km] out from Earth.
Velocity ever decreasing as we near the crossover point.
Total spacecraft weight still 102,890 pounds [46,670 kg].

We're continuing to leave the circuit live, background noise and all, as we anticipate resumption of communications between the spacecraft communicator Don Peterson and the crew of Apollo 16 for the day's activities.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Okay, Pete, I'm up on biomed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, the biomed data looks good.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: You can tell Charlie's still breathing, huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We've been suspecting it up here all along.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Houston, based on the burn yesterday, is the data you gave us for the SPS burn card still going to hold for LOI?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And the other question is, does it look like we're going to have another midcourse, and which one and how much is it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, we'll get back to you in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: On the burn card, we will probably have to do some more updating, we'll get to that tomorrow.
And on the midcourses, Midcourse 4, right now, is - looks like it's less than one foot per second.
If it gets much bigger, we'll probably do it.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: [Garble], Pete, you probably read us loud and clear in the [garble] mode [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Say again on all [garble] on the burn card.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We probably will have to do some updating on that - on that PAD.
And we'll let you know on that and we'll update it tomorrow.
And on the Midcourse 4 - Midcourse 4 is looking pretty small, less than one foot per second now.
If it gets larger, we'll probably go ahead and do it, but we'll keep you advised on it.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Houston, your up-link keeps fading in and out on you.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I read you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: You faded cut on your last transmission again.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, hang on a minute until the comm clears up a little bit and then I'll come back to you.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: On that updating the PAD, we'll get that to you tomorrow.
And right now, Midcourse 4 looks very small, less than one foot per second.
If it gets larger, we will probably do it, but we'll keep you advised on that.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Just to review that SPS pressure system - as near as I can make out, it's purely - we don't actually have a Delta-P.
It's just - of about more than five psi.
What we do have is a gauge difference.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: There's a - there's a chamber in there - a reference chamber that's supposed to be at atmospheric pressure.
It has apparently vented to a vacuum condition, which brings in about - about a 14.7-psi bias.
Then there's a 5-psi meter bias on top of that.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, so actually, the two meters, as we look at them now, are actually balanced.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, the actual pressures on both meters - the actual pressures are the same, although the meters are reading differently.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Will we need to charge Battery B, just looking ahead in the Flight Plan a little bit?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Charlie, you can go ahead and start charging the battery now.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, Pete, Bat B is on charge now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Finally had time to look out the window and you're just coming into view again and just as pretty as ever.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What you looking at right now?
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Can you tell what part of the Earth?
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Can you yell what part of the Earth you're looking at?
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Well, we got a half-Earth, and we see a land mass - see the North Pole - the north polar cap with two big swirls coming out off of it to the southwest.
I can see the subsolar point very distinctly.
It's a big white dot in the center towards the LM, away from the terminator.
And there's a big land mass that's visible in the center.
And can't really make out exactly what it is.
It's the only place that's fairly clear.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we show you directly over North Africa.
[Pause.] And that's just about the terminator.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, you got North Africa at the terminator?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, that's what it is, then, looking at it, yeah.
[Pause.] Looks like a good storm system up in the Atlantic Ocean and above the subsolar point.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The blue color and the white of the clouds and the blue of the ocean, Dick.
Things just stayed pretty constant as we move out - still really a spectacular sight.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What do they think of this PTC down there in the trenches?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I don't know who to congratulate, Ken or the computer.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I guess we'll give Ken credit for that one.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: 16, let us know what you think about the number of light flashes and whether or not it's feasible to try the ALFMED.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: That is, if you can work on seeing flashes.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're going to start on the ALFMED now.
I guess it'll take us about an hour and a half from the time we start to the time we finish up.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And you think you are seeing enough light flashes to make it valid?
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: They haven't gotten around to doing that yet, but we're working on it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Don, Charlie saw some this morning when he got up.
And so we're going to put the ALFMED on Charlie.
And we will not turn it on until we start to see some flashes, and if we don't see any, we'll just terminate it.
We'll talk it over with you.
We'll tell you when we're ready to go through it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Ken, we'll be standing by.

And, Ken, while I'm talking to you, we've got a little more information on this platform problem and its a fairly big mouthful, so, sometime when you get a chance where you can copy some information, give me a call.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: It looks like it'll be a couple of hours.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We don't want you to get into another P52 before we have time to talk about it.a16_0480622
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Pete, (clear's throat) a little background on these flashes [garble].
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We missed your comments on the light flashes.
You'd started to give them to us, and you were blocked out by noise.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I was just telling you - the first night - during the first sleep period, whatever that was GET, I saw numerous light flashes before going to sleep, probably a - as freq - as high as three or four a minute.
The next morning, they were not as numerous as that, and then last night still not as numerous.
This morning, I was perhaps seeing maybe one every couple of minutes or so.
If that frequency repeats itself during this test, is that good enough to go ahead?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: If you see one every minute or so, that's enough to go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: At 48:30 in the Flight Plan, there's a line that says "Synchronize mission timer to CMC clock," and it is not required.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Hey, Don, is our comm gonna be good enough the way we're going here now for you to get the marks or are we gonna - can we count on recording them on board too?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You better record on board as well.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: You'll start the tape recorder, is that affirmative ?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Or you want us to do that?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, if at any time, the voice quality goes down, our procedures, as I understand them described, will - I'll knock off or I'll take Charlie's place here, and I'll knock off the observations and go to recording.
And other than that, Charlie will be doing all the things that I was going to be doing.
But, if you want us to record them in pen and ink at the same time, just give us a call.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You all were so quiet, you scared us.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I just got the ALFMED on and in position, and we're gonna see if we see anything.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Stand by a minute; we'll look at that.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: We were just about ready to call this thing off and try it another day.
And then looked like Charlie was starting to see them at - at a pretty fair rate.
That five in 15 minutes was like four in the last minute or two.
And John has seen one; I have not seen any yet.
But sounds like Charlie is gonna be able to see these and I'm gonna go ahead and take the photographs and get - get all set and wait for your answer.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We've been advised that we do want to go ahead with the ALFMED experiment.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I'll tell you when we're ready to start timing.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: They seem to come in clusters.
You get one or two right after - and then - then nothing for a while.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Can you help us [garble] record our [garble] 12 [garble] 0.15, 1.2, [garble] at three feet shows Charlie in the ALFMED with his face sticking out.
I'm taking a picture from the LEB looking up towards the center couch.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: 16, that was so garbled we didn't copy, so you're gonna have to say it again.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: We were in - had a lot of background noise, and we didn't get any of that.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Did you copy comments about the film?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We had too much background noise.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I guess I'll write it down.
I think that's faster after all.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Could you make sure the tape recorder's running?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're going to rewind it, and I'll get it back to you in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: In the meantime, Ken, if you've got comments we'll take them on air-to-ground.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Does that mean you want us to go ahead and start?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Was that Roger or Stand by?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, a bright dot in the - in my right eye in the upper - upper left center.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Bright dot in the left eye, upper left - right center.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The first one was a fuzzy dot in the right eye - the left eye.
The second one was a bright dot in the right eye, lower left center.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Light streak - white streak in the right eye, upper center - moving from up - bottom to up - top.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Faint left - faint white dot in the left eye - extreme left.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Bright flash in upper left eye.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Apollo 16 crew now conducting the Apollo light flash experiment, using the blinders.
The Apollo ALFMED, Apollo Light Flash in Moving Emulsion Detector, is the acronym, ALFMED.
At 1:30 today, Central Standard Time, in the small briefing room in the Apollo News Center, MSC, there'll be a briefing by a panel of several scientist.
The topic is "Magnetic Enigmas of the Moon".
Spacecraft distance at the present time, 160,746 nautical miles [297,702 km].
At 49:17, still up live air-to-ground, this is Apollo Control.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Flash across the bottom of right eye - white dot - from right [garble] left to right but I doubt it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Simultaneous white dots in the right eye, upper and lower center.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: A bright flash in the lower center of the right eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: A little light dim streak in the lower right eye - lower left.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Lower left - left eye, in the outboard, and it was a bright flash - looked like a streak - probably going from right to left.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Bright dot, upper right, outboard, right eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: At about 049:25:10, the air/ground line is interrupted by a Spanish voice.
As the PAO announcer will shortly explain, this is a Spanish telephone engineer who is inadvertently connected to the circuit.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Dot - bright dot in the upper right eye, center.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I've also had some very subtle things that - just very - looks like little dim flashes that I haven't been calling.
They're just so subtle, I've been afraid to call them a definite mark.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Upper right eye going from left to right.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Charlie, if you see any more of the subtle marks, how about calling them and just call them a subtle mark.
We'd like to record that information.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: The mysterious comments and singing and frequent repetition of 'hello, hello' on the Public Affairs broadcast line a few moments ago has been identified by the Network controller as a telephone company technician in Spain who inadvertently somehow got patched over onto the network line coming from the tracking station on the air/ground circuit.
He has been isolated, electronically that is; and hopefully, we shouldn't have anymore of that sort of thing.
Continuing with the ALFMED experiment at 49:31.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Bright dot, right eye, center, upper.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: [Garble] Left, right, the last one, I got the impression it was moving toward me.
Why, I don't know, but it didn't - didn't [garble].
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Subtle one, very subtle on the left eye, outboard, upper.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Flash in the lower right eye, sort of a distant lightning flash.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Upper center of upper right eye - bright light flash.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Faint dot, lower left eye, inboard.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Flash, a - a light streak in the left eye, starting at the center going to the bottom right from upper - from upper - center down to right in movement.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Left eye, sort of subtle, towards inboard center, a white flash.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Subtle dot - white dot - in the inboard center of the right eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That was the center upper, white dot, left eye; both of those were in left eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Upper right - part of right eye, white flash, small.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Subtle white flash, inboard upper right eye, inboard corner, that was.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: White dot, center - center right; lower of the left eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: All at the same time Charlie did.
Only mine were in the lower left side and not the right.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Flash across the bottom of the left eye, high speed.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Can you tell us how we're doing on time?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Stand by one; I'll let you know.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Don, this thing was made for about a size 6¼ or something head.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'll make a note of that.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I'm not complaining, it's just a little tight.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Bright flash down at the right center of the right eye.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: A dull fuzzy spot in the outboard center of the left eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Flash in the center of the right eye - sort of like a small flash.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Flash in the center of left eye, just a faint flash - much more like a dot.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: White dot, outboard, right eye, center.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Small flash in the lower center of the right eye.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Very tiny flash in the lower center of the left eye.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Streak across the lower left of the left eye, on the inboard side.
The light was going to left - from right to left.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Two little dot flashes in the upper right center of the right eye - left eye, excuse me.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Small flash in the upper right center of the [garble].
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: My last mark was the upper center right eye, little flash.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Small flash in the left center of the right eye.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: A - one - the first one was right eye, a streak going from inboard center to the upper right.
Left one was a streak, pencil shaped - a pencil line, left eye from center to upper right.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Very subtle flash in the light - right eye, upper cen - upper right.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Another subtle one just below that, center right, right eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Left eye, a streak starting in the center.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The left eye was a streak starting in the center going out to the right.
It increased in size as it went from right to left.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Subtle dot, outboard side, center left eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Faint dot in the right eye, center, upper, and the inboard side is a white dot in the left eye - and the second one - was in the inboard side, center.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Streak in the lower left of - left eye, moving from top to bottom.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Simultaneous white dots, right and left eye.
Left one was in the upper right, inboard center.
Left - right one was in the inboard bottom, left.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Subtle flash in the right eye, upper inboard.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Flash in the right eye, right side.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: 16, we figure the time's about up on the ALFMED experiment.
We'd like to know if the motor's stopped.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: A streak in the lower left side of the left eye, moving down.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Keep going until the motor stops.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Thin white dot upper right, inboard, left eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Lightning-flash-type phenomena, lower right, inboard, left eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: White dot, left eye, outboard, center.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Right eye, center, inboard, a white dot.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Ken, we've got all the ALFMED data we need.
We want you to give us a mark when you shut the motor off.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Whenever you can copy, I've got some words on the IMU problem that we had.
Also some - an entry to the G&C Checklist.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: How about standing by, would you?
Stand by until we clean up all the [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You're cutting out pretty bad, Don.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: 16, the PI advises on the ALFMED experiment that we got about 70 counts, and he's very happy with the results, and he wants you to verify that the clutch is in the Stow position.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Those things - those things are really something, Don.
There were a couple of the phenomena that I had seen previously that I didn't see today, but there were some other ones today that were different too.
The phenomena - the flashes leave no afterglow and they're - they're just instantaneous.
All the colors are white - were all we saw.
We saw no colors at all; neither John nor I.
It was - every one we saw was white.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm ready to copy some things now.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Before we get into copying stuff, let me confirm what Charlie said about that clutch.
Now I listened to that thing retract before, and as soon as we went to the mid-position on the clutch - why, you can hear the plates come down.
And we went ahead and rapped it once, anyhow, just to be sure, but I didn't hear anything jiggle.
And we cycled it to Operate and then back to Stow, and it all felt normal and the - the plate travel sounded proper.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, and I guess my only comment on ALFMED is that I think those light flashes are made by the same guy that makes the emperor's clothes.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I think they're made by the same guy that makes the emperor's clothes.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Hey, Don, I'm standing by to copy whatever words you got about P52s and G&N and any of those other subjects pertaining to our operation.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Ken, I'll - we'll talk about the platform problem first, and I'm gonna back up and talk about the original data that we passed up because there are some small corrections to that.
And then I'll - I'll try to break it down into about four shovelfuls here for you, because it's a fairly big mouthful if we try to do it all at once.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: You have some stuff that I probably should copy, huh?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Yes, I will have some items for you to copy.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: We're coming up on an omni switch.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, we'll start here on this thing.
First of all, the - the problem occurs, apparently, in CSM-117 when the TVC relay changes state, either going from enable to disable or from disable to enable.
And it causes an electric glitch that makes the CDUs go to 90 degrees; in particular, the yaw CDU, and therefore the CMC thinks it's in gimbal lock and goes into a coarse align mode.

The - some of the cases that could cause this, for example, are when you go to - when you select Manual Optics, you have the TVC relay enabled and then if you go from Manual to Auto, or if you hit the Zero Optics switch with Manual Optics selected, or if you go from P52 to P00 with the Manual Optics selected, you will reset the TVC relay, and that can cause the glitch.
This is apparently what happened last night.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, is it important for me to copy those things that cause this, or are you going to give me a way to prevent it?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You don't have to copy those.
Now we're going to talk about what you can do here in P52s and maybe subsequently in - for the LOI and for P40s.
There are two ways to approach the P52.
There's a - there's a quick and dirty way which is to simply go to SCS Control, because with SCS control, the TVC relay is not enabled.
And, by doing that, you don't run the risk of generating this glitch.
That's not the way, however, that the guys are recommending, because they have a procedure that they think will handle not only the P52, but the burn cases.
And they'd like you to get that procedure, and you're going to have to copy that one.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: How about running down that list of things that causes the TVC enable to change state again?
Let me - let me copy those this time.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And - now - these cases are not all inclusive.
These are just some examples that - that we can bring up to you.
First of all, the TVC relay is enabled when you select Manual Optics.

It is then subsequently disabled if you go from Manual to Auto or if you go to zero the Optics with Manual selected, or if you go with Manual - with the Manual Optics selected, if you go from P52 to P00.
But these are probably not the only cases; they're probably just some examples that we know of.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Another important point, Ken, is that it changes state during the TVC gimbal drive check.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Yeah, that's what I was afraid of.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're going to - this next procedure that I have to read up to you is probably going to be the one that we'll use to try to get around that.
And also, we'd like you to use it in the P52s because it allows us to monitor for the glitch, and at the same time prevents the glitch from bringing your platform down.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, if you're ready to copy...
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: ...[garble] copy this or - Okay, I'll just copy this on a scratch pad, and we'll put it in the appropriate place, if that sounds reasonable.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Number 1, we want you to key Verb 48 Enter and load Noun 46, Register 1.
The first digit should be loaded as a 3.
The rest of the numbers can be left as they are.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, why don't you read on at about that pace, Don, and I'll just copy, and then I'll read it back to you?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, the second step is key Verb 25 Noun 07 Enter, 75 Enter, 1 Enter, 1 Enter.
That sets the Average g flag, but does not turn Average g on.
The combination of those two will prevent the CMC from going into coarse align.
[Pause.] After you've done that, you can select P52 and use your normal alignment procedures.
[Long pause.] And, when you've completed the P52 to terminate this - EMP, we'll have you key Verb 48 Enter, load Noun 4 Register 1 with its original value, whether that was 2 or 1 in the first digit.
Step 2, we'll have you a key Verb 25 Noun 07 Enter, 75 Enter, 1 Enter, Enter.
[Pause.] That'll then return your - you to the correct DAP and also reset the Average g flag.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, that - that was, in Verb 25 Noun 07, that was a 75 Enter, 1 Enter, and then zero Enter or a 1 Enter?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, let me look it over here for a second.
At first glance, the first thing I see here looks like - do I - should I [garble]?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: 16, we're switching omnis; hold off a minute.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: You can go ahead now with the readback on that any time you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Did you get my question about the Verb 46?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Negative, but we have a caution note that says, "Do not key Verb 46 Enter while this EMP is in to prevent Sap - Saturn DAP actually coming on."
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, what you would do then is - we're gonna set Saturn DAP in Average g in order to prevent coarse aligning in the event the middle gimbal picks up spurious signals.
And to do this, we set Verb 48 with a Noun 46.
Digit A is set to a 3, and we set the Average g flag with a Verb 25 Noun 07, address 75, bit 1, set it to a 1.
Then we should call P52 normally, and if the glitch occurs, it'll be ignored.
I assume from this that if the glitch occurs sometime while we're in the process of taking marks, that we get some kind of garbage out of P52, but it should be obvious.
And from what you gave me on this list up at the top, it looks like normally we would not run across TVC Enable during that period.
At the completion of a 52, we go back into a Verb 48, and now we take Noun 46 back to its original values.
Then we reset the Average g flag by taking channel 75 bit 1 to a zero, using Verb 25 Noun 07, and the restrictions on this EMP is that we will not use Verb 46 at any time while this is punched in.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, do you - do you have some words on how - on how we handle the P40?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Ken, and - in the P40 is what we're thinking about, and we haven't completely decided now, is that we'll probably use this EMP before and after the TVC gimbal check, and we may move the TVC gimbal check earlier or before the burn so that we have plenty of time to get this thing in and out.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, that sounds like a good plan.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, now I've got the last shovelful here if you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, I've got my bucket out.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, if you should lose the platform alignment, we'll do the same thing we tried last night, a Verb 23 Noun 20 Enter, Enter, Verb 40 Enter, and then go to the checklist, G/7-1, and do the rapid IMU realign.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: If we - if we tumble the thing again, we're gonna release the platform by setting Noun 20 to - to zeros, and we do that with the Verb 23 Noun 20 Enter, Enter.
Then we release the platform with the Verb 40 Enter, and then we go through page G/7-1 and get a rapid alignment to the GDC.
Now that - that assumes that what happened to us was the glitch in the middle gimbal.
If I understood your conversation, it's possible to have a glitch in one of the other gimbals.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That will not, however, get you into the - into the problem that causes the platform to go into coarse align.
And also, we were advised that while this EMP is in, you shouldn't have the problem with losing the platform alignment.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: If we - if we get this glitch in one of the other axes - I - and we have the controls enabled, I assume one of the things we would - we'd see would be some unusual thruster activity.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That's affirmative, and in that case I guess all you need...
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Well, I'm trying to figure out what we'd do if we have a - say we're in attitude hold.
If we have that glitch occur right now and - with our engines disabled, I don't think there would be any way I'd see it until I went to use some auto optics or something.

And that - if we were in an attitude hold, it appears to me that I would see a - a DAP that appeared to be unstable in the axis that it was going for, or at least it would take off for some place other than where it is.
And we would go to SCS momentarily, and the proper response would be a Verb 40, Enter, and allow the CMC to zero and recount the IMU CDUs.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: This TVC enable that we're talking about that's used for the gimbal drive test - now that's a function only of the gimbal drive test and not a function of the setting of the trims in P40.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, so, our P40 then would be something like we would go out and we'll get a gimbal drive check to you folks early, then we'll turn the gimbals back off probably, and I'm just reiterating what I'm [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I'm trying to give you my impression of what we're gonna be - the general approach to the burn - and we check out the gimbal drives and the gimbal trims, then when we come into P40 to do it for the actual burn, we would go ahead and use the SCS gimbal checks to verify all the gimbals are properly hooked up.
Then we would bypass the flashing 202 with an Enter, and we'd just let the gimbals go to trim, and everything else would be done nominally.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, I think your general impression's right.
I guess we're not yet ready to commit ourselves to a - a set of procedures.
We're going to have Stu take a look at it in the simulator here, and we'll come up to you later with some detailed procedures specifically for the burn.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I understand that; I just wanted to make sure I had a general understanding in case we had to do some original thinking.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I think we concur with what you said so far.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: And the other thing, that's out of curiosity - if the guys in the back rooms, after they get through getting all the important things squared away, that they could kind of think some more about their list of things that causes this enable relay to change state.
I'll compile a list of those things in case we come across something later on that we hadn't thought about.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Ken, we'll do that for you.
And there's one last note here, that, prior to your P52s, it'd be a good idea to align the GDC just prior to going in.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I don't think I'll let that guy get very far away from us.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The GDCs (Gyro Display Couplers) are part of the secondary system that keeps track of the spacecraft's attitude (the IMU and its associated couplers being primary).
The gyros used for the secondary system are 'strapped down'; that is they are not mounted on a stabilised platform but instead are fixed to the spacecraft's body.
They are known as Body Mounted Attitude Gyros (BMAGs).
They don't measure absolute attitude but rather measure the rate of rotation.
Absolute attitude can be derived from these measurements, a job that is carried out by the GDCs.
However, this only works if they have a known starting point, usually given to them by the primary system using the GDC Align button.

The attitude measurement given by the GDCs is much more prone to drift than the primary system so if Ken is to use the GDCs to realign a misaligned IMU platform, it would be a good idea for him to press the GDC Align button just prior to starting any task that might cause the IMU to becomes misaligned.
That way, any drift of the GDCs will be minimised.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: And, Ken, I've got an update to the G&C Checklist on page 9-4 any time you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, on Page 9-4, line 07, column A ...
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Column A, line 07, should read '76747.' Line 11 should read '77552.' Line 12, '77756'; line 13, '77307.' [Long pause.]
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, I 'm on page G/9-4, reading down column A.
On line 7, I replace '77426' with '76747.' On line 11, I replace '00214' with '77552.' On line 12, I replace '77714' with '77756.' Line 13, I replace '77446' with '77307.'
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And we've got, Ken, here some notes on this jet-on monitor EMP.
We'd like to do a check on it at 54:25, and I can read you up the procedure for that any time you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, at 54:25 in the Flight Plan, we would like to add 'P20 option 5,' in the 'LM checkout attitude', Noun 78, minus 09000, minus 03000, plus 25500; Noun 79, plus 00050; Noun 70, plus 00047.
We will up link the jet-on monitor loads and, when the P20 maneuver's complete, you can do a Verb 74 Enter.
[Pause.] And the P20 attitude that we've selected will be the attitude that you're already in, so there is actually not a maneuver involved here.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, when you say when maneuver complete, - does that mean that you would want us to maneuver to the LM checkout attitude using P20, or are you going to let us go to Verb 49 to that?
And then, I'll call it up and bypass the maneuver, is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You can use a Verb 49 for that, Ken.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: A Verb 49, and then we'll call P20, and then you can command the same attitude I'm in.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: In other words, I've got the 50 18 the second time.
Then you want the Verb 74, Enter.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Coming up on an omni switch, 16.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, the only other thing is at 56 hours, that's 56:00, we want you to terminate the jet-on monitor EMP.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, can you tell me what deadband you're gonna be setting in there?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Ken, it'll be a one-degree deadband.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, you're setting me in the EMP at one-degree deadband; is that affirm?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, on the Noun 70, I read you a - a plus 00047.
You actually don't need that plus; that's an octal number.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, it's not clear to me - if we're going to exceed this or not; we normally would not.
Do you want to try letting us drift out of the deadband and see if the monitor works?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Negative; that's - that's not the intent, I think.
They just want to get the program in and look at it.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Well, sometime before we get through, would you ask them if they would object to letting us see if that thing triggers the same response that we're used to?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Ken; we'd like to think that over.
We'll get back to you on that.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Now I've still got this note that we've had since about 24 hours ago on changing the angle on that Sun wheel and if you have that out now, we can clean that up otherwise, I'll hold it a while.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I don't have it out, but why don't you tell me what it is.
I think I know what you're talking about.
It's due to the REFSMMAT angles on there being changed.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And we [belch] inked '27 degrees' on it.
We should have written in '37 degrees'.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: It turns out we're at - is inked in at 27 and it should be 37.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: What's the probability of this - glitch happening?
Is it about one in a million?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: 16, I guess we really don't know the answer to that.
That's one of the reasons we want to get this EMP in to kind of watch for it and see if it's occurring frequently or infrequently.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, Don, we're going to go ahead and punch through this P52 and we'll start with our little procedure here, and you might kind of watch us through and make sure we do it right the first time.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're watching and you can go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, and what did you say the probability was of this thing happening?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think we don't know the answer to that, John.
That's why we want to load this software program so we can monitor to see if that glitch is occurring frequently - or it may never occur again, we really don't know.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: And you got the GDC aligned there?
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Houston, how do you like those angles?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Do you want us to torque some that small?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: [Pause.] Yeah, I guess that's 51:18, excuse me.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, would you like for me to return it to zero with the switch in Manual this time, just to see if you get that glitch?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, 16, we didn't see any glitch.
[Pause.] There, we're coming up on an omni switch.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: And, 16, that EMP that we loaded to protect the platform - we'll refer to it as EMP 509.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: 16, if you'll go Accept, we've got an uplink for you.
And I've got news, if you're interested.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Former President Lyndon Johnson is resting comfortably after he was hospitalized again following an increase in his heart rate.
Vice President Agnew, speaking here in Houston, has asked the nation's supermarket executives to hold the line on prices.
If prices for food continue to soar, Mr.
Agnew hinted at mandatory Federal controls.
President Nixon added a stop during his scheduled trip to Moscow.
Prior to returning home, he will stop off in Poland to discuss Polish-American relations.
The Moscow trip in late May is still on, despite some fears that recent developments in the Vietnamese conflict could affect the President's visit to the Soviet Union.
State news: The Texas governor's race is still a hot item, but no word yet from John Connally, who said he may speak out on the wide-open race.
Congressman Bob Eckhardt says the U.S.
needs at least three large super ocean ports to keep in contention with the world market.
He says the Galveston-Houston area is a prime superport site.
And on sports news: The Astros captured their first victory of the year with a 7-2 win over the L.A.
And some scores on the other games: in the American League; Cleveland 4, Boston 0; Baltimore 4, New York 0.
The Boston marathon, run yesterday on Patriots Day in Beantown, was won by a 25-year-old engineering student from Finland.
Strangely enough, they've omitted his name.
A 33-year-old Long Island, New York, woman, Nina Kuscsik, won the special ladies division.
The Los Angeles Lakers and the Milwaukee Bucks are tied 2-2 in their Western division playoffs in the NBA.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Charlie wants to know how (laughter) - how Consolidated Jack Pine is doing.
transcript.speaker: Young

transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: [Pause.] Charlie, I guess I haven't got those figures handy.

tokens: This is Apollo Control; 52 hours, 17 minutes Ground Elapsed Time.
Apollo 16 now being maneuvered into a High Gain Antenna attitude leaving the Passive Thermal Control after all night and most of the day.

Midcourse correction 3 will not be performed and it's likely that Midcourse 4 will not be either, in as much as the present calculations show that the correction would about 1.4 feet per second [0.4 metres per second] at the present time.
We should be getting some improvement in communications when we do get locked up on the High Gain Antenna.
And we're continuing to stand by at 52:18.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Let me tell you, all our plans for peanut butter were - were correct.

tokens: [Pause.] You guys gonna do the Skylab thing tonight?

It's like when we were aboard ship, we can get that peanut butter sandwich when we're in too much of a hurry to do something else - to do anything else, and it works!

transcript.speaker: Peterson

transcript.speaker: Peterson

transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: [Pause.] Kind of down over the tip of ...
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: 16, could you verify the position of the S-Band Aux TV switch?
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Yeah, it's still in SCI, waiting for you guys to tell us to finish up our film cycling checklist.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You can take that switch to Off, but leave the Pan Camera Heaters and stuff on.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Well, since we - you don't want us to do the exact checklist, we'll - we'll turn the SCI, Off and the Data System, Off, or do you want the Data System On?
You're not reading anything now, I don't think.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Just leave the Data System, On.
We've commanded it Off on the ground.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Houston, with the binocs out looking at the Earth, we can see Florida and the - the real blue water around the Bahamas.
On around the Gulf of Mexico, looks like you might have some clouds near Houston and on down into Mexico and the Great Lakes up in the North, where there's lots of clouds on up north of that.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: [Garble] and up in the polar icecap, there is a big broken line; it looks like a river or something running down off to the southwest.
Wonder what that feature might be?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Does it look like - Is it a feature on the ground or a feature in the clouds?
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Well, I cau - I thought the whole thing was just snow and ice up there; maybe it is the clouds up in - it looked like to me it's - it's just up at the North Pole in the icecap area.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And it's been there since we laun - it's been there since we launched.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What it appeared to me to be was some places thawed out up there, but - and I was looking at water, but that might be the clouds actually.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll see if he knows anything about it.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, and that storm system that was out west of Alaska or thereabouts appears to be still there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm ready to copy the rest of those things that reset the TVC [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, stand by just a minute.
They're adding a couple of notes to it.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: [Pause.] Can I read back the other things when I read back the ones that set it?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I'll just wait until you get it all; I was going to read back the one that set it.
We pulled those things out in Earth orbit as we were going over the States, and it's pretty impressive.
It works out well in other places; it really does enhance things you can see.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, I've got this list on the TVC Relay set/reset conditions again, if you want to go back into that now.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: The note they added was back up on the very first thing I gave you: the number 1, conditions to set the relay.
Those conditions only work in certain programs or extended verbs, and those are P20, 22, 23, 24, 52, or Verb 41 Noun 91.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, and I believe we'd gotten down through numbers...
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Is it all options to P20?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Stand by a minute, Ken; we'll get you an answer on that.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: While we're waiting for them to decide whether it's all options or not, I believe we got down through number 2, reset conditions.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I copied the Optics to Zero and the Optics Zero to Off when in CMC Mode control.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Number 3 is Spacecraft Control switch, SCS.
[Pause.] Number 5 is Verb 37 Enter, XX Enter.
[Pause.] Number 7 is Verb 34 or Pro in the sighting mark routine, R53 display.
[Pause.] And number 8 is Auto Enter RCS DAP at SPS cut-off plus 2.5 seconds in P40.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: I didn't understand that one, Don.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: It's the - it's what happens to you immediately following the burns.
The SPS cuts off, and then 2.5 seconds later in P40, the TVC relay gets reset.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, and on the question of options, back up under set condition number 1, it's all options except number 2 in program 20.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, and I understand that this - this can happen when you either set or reset the enable relay, is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: They say it can happen going - any change of state on the TVC relay.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Can you tell me if it's the change of state of the relay, or just the command to change?

For instance, if we already had it in some of these things like a Verb 37, any program resets it; but if it's already in the reset position, is that a condition that's likely to trigger one of these things, or is that one of the safe conditions?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We - we think, Ken, it's the actual relay set/reset changing, not - not the command.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Don, let me read back what you read to me, and then I'll want to mull that over for a while and see if I have any other questions.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, and I think that, except the TVC enable [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, the things that will set the TVC enable are the Spacecraft Control switch to CMC, the Optic Zero to Off, the Optics Mode to Manual, and the CMC TVC gimbal test in P40.
These things occur only if I'm in P20 options 0, 1, 3, 4; P22, P23, P24, P52; and a Verb 41 Noun 91.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: On that, the first three of those constitute one set of conditions; and the last one, that is, the start of CMC thrust vector control, is another condition that is sufficient by itself.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: The first three go with those programs, and the CMC thrust vector control is sufficient by itself.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Did you - I don't remember if we concluded whether that was when it - the CMC sets the gimbal in preparation for the burn or only during that test.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I don't think that's - at least, I haven't been advised as to which set of conditions we're talking about there.
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: Okay, now I'll read you the reset ones.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: It's Optics to Zero, Optics Zero to Off when in CMC Mode, Spacecraft Control to SCS, Translation Hand Controller to clockwise, any Verb 37 Enter to XX Enter, a fresh start, a Verb 34 or Pro when in the sighting mark display, and the Auto engine off or Auto TVC reset after a burn in P40.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: [Pause.] There are eight different reset conditions; number 2 actually contains two separate items [Pause.].
transcript.speaker: Peterson

tokens: We're starting the fuel cell purge and waste water dump.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, we've got a LM/CM Delta-P of 0.6.
We'd like to go ahead and pressurize the cabin and get ready for the LM entry.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We copied, and you can go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Peterson
